title: The Simple Future (Futuro Simple)
description: Form the future by adding one set of endings (-é, -ás, -á, -emos, -éis, -án) to the WHOLE infinitive — hablaré, comeré, viviré. Learn the common irregular stems (tendr-, podr-, har-, dir-, saldr-...), the uses (predictions, promises) and the future of probability (¿Dónde estará Juan?).

cards.front: hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablaréis, hablarán
cards.back: simple future of regular verbs (hablar = to speak)
cards.notes: One set of endings for **all** verbs: **-é, -ás, -á, -emos, -éis, -án**. They attach to the **whole infinitive**, not a stem: *hablar* + *é* = **hablaré** (I will speak). All forms carry a written accent except *nosotros* (*hablaremos*).

cards.back: -ER and -IR take the same future endings as -AR
cards.notes: Unlike the present, the future uses **one** set of endings for *-AR, -ER* and *-IR*. The base is always the **full infinitive**: *comer* → **comeré** (not *comé*), *vivir* → **viviré**. *Comeré más tarde* = I will eat later.

cards.front: Saldré. = I will leave.  ✗ Yo will salir.
cards.back: no separate word for 'will' — the ending carries it
cards.notes: English uses a helper word *will* (*I will go*). Spanish has **no** word for *will* — the future ending alone carries that meaning: *Iré* = I will go. Never add a separate word for *will*.

cards.front: tendré, tendrás, tendrá, tendremos, tendréis, tendrán
cards.back: future of tener (to have) — stem tendr-
cards.notes: *tener* drops the *e* and adds a *d*: stem **tendr-**. The endings are the same: *tendré, tendrás...* *Tendré tiempo mañana* = I'll have time tomorrow. Same pattern: *poner* → **pondr-**, *salir* → **saldr-**, *venir* → **vendr-**.

cards.back: stems that just drop a vowel: poder, saber, querer, haber
cards.notes: These lose the infinitive vowel: *poder* → **podr-**, *saber* → **sabr-**, *querer* → **querr-** (double r), *haber* → **habr-**. E.g. *Podré ir* = I will be able to go; *Habrá una fiesta* = There will be a party.

cards.back: the two short stems: hacer → har-, decir → dir-
cards.notes: *hacer* and *decir* are extra short: stems **har-** and **dir-**. *Haré la cena* = I'll make dinner; *Te diré la verdad* = I'll tell you the truth. Don't say *haceré* or *deciré*.
Like *tener*, these drop a vowel and insert a **d**: *salir* → **saldr-**, *poner* → **pondr-**, *venir* → **vendr-**. *Saldré pronto* = I'll leave soon; *Vendrán mañana* = They'll come tomorrow.

cards.front: Mañana lloverá. Te llamaré. España ganará.
cards.notes: The simple future expresses **future events** (*Mañana lloverá* = It will rain tomorrow), **promises** (*Te llamaré* = I'll call you), and **predictions** (*España ganará* = Spain will win).

The future also expresses a **guess about the present**: *¿Dónde estará Juan?* = Where can Juan be? / I wonder where Juan is. *Serán las tres* = It must be about three o'clock. This is conjecture, not a future time.

cards.back: ir a + infinitive (near future) vs futuro simple
cards.notes: A1's **ir a + infinitive** (*Voy a estudiar* = I'm going to study) is common for near, planned actions. The **futuro simple** (*Estudiaré*) is a bit more formal/distant. Both are correct and widely used.

cards.front: mañana, la próxima semana, el año que viene
cards.back: time markers that point to the future
cards.notes: Words that signal the future: *mañana* (tomorrow), *la próxima semana* (next week), *el año que viene* (next year), *luego* (later), *pronto* (soon). *El año que viene viviremos en Madrid.*

cards.back: endings attach to the WHOLE infinitive, not a stem
cards.notes: For regular verbs, **keep the full infinitive** and add the ending: *comer* + *é* = **comeré**. Do not cut off *-er/-ar/-ir* first. *comé* and *hablé* are preterite forms, not future.

steps.title: One set of endings, on the whole infinitive
steps.body:
# The Simple Future

The **futuro simple** is one of the easiest Spanish tenses to form. There is just **one** set of endings, and for regular verbs you add them to the **entire infinitive** — you do **not** chop off *-ar/-er/-ir* first.

| | hablar | comer | vivir |
|---|---|---|---|
| yo | hablaré | comeré | viviré |
| tú | hablarás | comerás | vivirás |
| él/ella | hablará | comerá | vivirá |
| nosotros | hablaremos | comeremos | viviremos |
| vosotros | hablaréis | comeréis | viviréis |
| ellos | hablarán | comerán | vivirán |

The endings are **-é, -ás, -á, -emos, -éis, -án**. Every form has a written accent **except** *nosotros* (*hablaremos*).

> ⚠️ **Whole infinitive, not a stem.** It's *comer* → **comeré**, never *comé*. Cutting the verb gives you a preterite form by accident.

> ⚠️ **No word for "will".** English needs a helper (*I will eat*). Spanish doesn't — the ending alone means *will*: *comeré* = I will eat.

steps.title: The irregular stems
steps.body:
## A small group of irregular stems

The **endings never change**. A handful of common verbs simply use an irregular **stem** instead of the full infinitive. They fall into three neat groups:

**1. Drop a vowel:**

| Verb | Stem | yo form |
|---|---|---|
| poder | podr- | podré |
| saber | sabr- | sabré |
| querer | querr- | querré |
| haber | habr- | habrá (there will be) |

**2. Drop a vowel and insert a *d*:**

| Verb | Stem | yo form |
|---|---|---|
| tener | tendr- | tendré |
| poner | pondr- | pondré |
| salir | saldr- | saldré |
| venir | vendr- | vendré |

**3. Two short ones:**

| Verb | Stem | yo form |
|---|---|---|
| hacer | har- | haré |
| decir | dir- | diré |

So: *Tendré tiempo* (I'll have time), *Podrás venir* (you'll be able to come), *Haré la cena* (I'll make dinner), *Te diré la verdad* (I'll tell you the truth).

steps.title: Uses, probability, and ir a + infinitive
steps.body:
## What the future is for

**Future events, promises, predictions:**
- *Mañana lloverá.* — It will rain tomorrow.
- *Te llamaré esta noche.* — I'll call you tonight. (promise)
- *España ganará el partido.* — Spain will win the match. (prediction)

**Future of probability (conjecture).** The same forms express a **guess about the present** — English uses *must be*, *probably*, or *I wonder*:
- *¿Dónde estará Juan?* — Where can Juan be? / I wonder where Juan is.
- *Serán las tres.* — It must be about three o'clock.
- *Estará en casa.* — He's probably at home.

## Futuro simple vs *ir a + infinitive*

In A1 you learned the **near future**: *ir a + infinitive*.

| ir a + infinitive | futuro simple |
|---|---|
| *Voy a estudiar.* | *Estudiaré.* |
| near, planned, conversational | a bit more formal / distant |

Both are common and often interchangeable. Choose *ir a* for immediate plans and the *futuro simple* for more distant or formal statements — and use the futuro simple for **probability**, where *ir a* does not work.

exercise.prompt: Match each subject with the correct simple-future form of *hablar* (to speak).

exercise.hint: comer + é = comeré (keep the whole infinitive). 'later' = más tarde.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *They will live in Madrid next year.*
accept: Vivirán en Madrid el año que viene.
vivirán en madrid el año que viene
Viviran en Madrid el ano que viene.
Ellos vivirán en Madrid el año que viene.
Vivirán en Madrid el próximo año.
exercise.hint: vivir + án = vivirán. 'next year' = el año que viene.
distractors: Vivrán en Madrid el año que viene.
Viván en Madrid el año que viene.
Viviran en Madrid el año que viene.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the yo simple-future of *tener*.
exercise.hint: tener uses the stem tendr- → tendré.

steps.title: Build: I will make dinner tonight

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I will make the dinner tonight.* (hacer)
exercise.hint: hacer has the short stem har- → haré. There is no separate word for 'will'.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *We will be able to come tomorrow.* (poder)
exercise.hint: poder → stem podr-, nosotros = podremos. 'to come' = venir.

exercise.prompt: You don't know where Juan is and you wonder aloud. Which sentence means 'Where can Juan be? / I wonder where Juan is'?
exercise.hint: The future form (estará) here expresses a guess about the present.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the yo simple-future of *decir*.
exercise.hint: decir has the short stem dir- → diré (not deciré).
salir → stem saldr- → saldré. There is no separate word for 'will'.

exercise.prompt: Match each verb with its irregular future stem.
Rewrite the near future as the simple future. *Voy a estudiar* → I ___ (estudiar) tonight.
exercise.hint: estudiar + é = estudiaré (the more formal equivalent of 'voy a estudiar').
